title: betula alba var. parvifolia
intro: Betula alba var. parvifolia, also known as the narrow-leaved birch, is a variety of the white birch tree that is native to the mountainous regions of central Asia. The leaves of the narrow-leaved birch have been used in traditional medicine for their anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which can help to soothe skin irritations and reduce fever. The bark of the tree has been used to treat various health conditions, including eczema, acne, and wounds, due to its astringent and antiseptic properties, which can help to promote healing and reduce inflammation. Narrow-leaved birch can be prepared as a herbal tea, infusion, or tincture, and is often used in combination with other herbs to enhance its medicinal properties.
